definition: The rate of change of velocity per unit of time.
The study of the properties of moving air, and especially of the interaction between the air and solid bodies moving through it.

meaning: The size of a sound wave, which determines how loud a sound is.
definition: The maximum extent of a vibration or oscillation, measured from the position of equilibrium.
example: Increasing the amplitude of the signal makes the music louder.

definition: Moving or tending to move away from a center.
Moving or tending to move toward a center.
The moving of something from its place or position.
The longitudinal retarding force exerted by air or other fluid surrounding a moving object.

meaning: A push or pull on an object that can change its motion or shape
definition: à¤¬à¤²
example: Gravity is a force that pulls objects toward the Earth.

meaning: The speed at which sound waves vibrate, which decides how high or low a pitch is.
definition: The rate at which something occurs or is repeated over a particular period of time or in a given sample.
example: Humans cannot hear sounds that vibrate at a very high frequency.

meaning: The natural force that attracts objects toward the center of the Earth or any other massive body
example: Gravity keeps the Moon orbiting around the Earth.

definition: A property of matter by which it continues in its existing state of rest or uniform motion in a straight line, unless that state is changed by an external force.
The branch of mechanics concerned with the motion of objects without reference to the forces which cause the motion.
An upward force acting on an object moving through a fluid.
A coherent, typically large body of matter with no definite shape.
example: The mass of the rock was recorded in kilograms.

definition: The quantity of motion of a moving body, measured as a product of its mass and velocity.
The SI unit of force.
Movement back and forth at a regular speed.
A weight hung from a fixed point so that it can swing freely backward and forward.
The rate of doing work or transferring energy.
Continuous physical force exerted on or against an object by something in contact with it.
A missile designed to be fired from a rocket or gun.

meaning: The quality of a sound being deep, full, and echoing, lasting for a short time.
definition: The reinforcement or prolongation of sound by reflection from a surface or by the synchronous vibration of a neighboring object.
example: The wooden body of the acoustic guitar gives it a warm resonance.
The car was running at a high speed.

definition: A sudden or violent lunge with a pointed weapon or a bodily part.
A twisting force that causes rotation.

meaning: The course of development over time (strong alternative to 'path' or 'direction').
definition: The path followed by a projectile flying or an object moving under the action of given forces.
example: The company's growth trajectory has been consistently upward for three years.

definition: The speed of something in a given direction.
The distance between successive crests of a wave, especially points in a sound wave or electromagnetic wave.

example: What is the weight of this bag?

definition: Activity involving mental or physical effort.
example: The solution seems to work well.
